content:
# Swishy data visualization templates - Overview

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart
Evidence: summary_only / medium
Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

Visual traits:
- animated chart
- count-up
- trend reveal
- spring bars
- glow metrics
- data storytelling

Use when:
- fintech dashboard
- metrics card
- MRR chart
- GitHub stars
- security dashboard
- progress chart

Avoid when:
- real-time critical monitoring
- medical safety dashboard
- very dense BI grid

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.

# Swishy data visualization templates - Layout Rules

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart
Evidence: summary_only / medium
Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

- Animate data to explain change, not just decorate it.
- Reveal axes/labels before or alongside values so the chart remains understandable.
- Use count-up and bar growth for key metrics; avoid looping chart animations after the initial explanation.

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.

# Swishy data visualization templates - Component Patterns

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart
Evidence: summary_only / medium
Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

- bar chart
- line trend
- countdown
- count-up metric
- progress ring
- GitHub stars trend
- financial card

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.

# Swishy data visualization templates - Interaction States

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart

Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

- load

- settled

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.

# Swishy data visualization templates - Accessibility Notes

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart
Evidence: summary_only / medium
Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

- Do not rely on animation to communicate the only data value.
- Provide readable labels and static values after animation ends.
- Respect reduced motion by skipping growth transitions.

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.

# Swishy data visualization templates - Implementation Hints

Page type: dashboard
Use cases: fintech dashboard, metrics card, MRR chart, GitHub stars, security dashboard, progress chart
Evidence: summary_only / medium
Source: https://www.swishy.ai/

- Use 500-900ms chart growth and 40-100ms stagger between bars.
- For financial dashboards, keep motion confident and precise rather than bouncy.
- Pair with glass cards by animating inner chart elements while the card surface remains stable.

AI summary: Use Swishy-style data visualization motion for dashboard storytelling: count-up metrics, bar growth, line/trend draw, progress rings, and staggered chart reveal, then settle to readable static values.
